top_signals.title: OPEC Cuts Demand Forecast Again as the Oil Market Starts Looking Past Hormuz
top_signals.summary: OPEC has trimmed its 2026 global oil demand growth forecast for the third straight month, even as crude production rebounds across the Gulf and tanker traffic slowly returns to the Strait of Hormuz. In its monthly oil market report released Monday, OPEC lowered expected oil demand growth this year to 780,000 barrels per day, down another 190,000 bpd from last month's forecast. The producer group still expects stronger consumption than many other forecasters, including the International Energy Agency, and even raised its demand growth estimate for…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Wells Fargo’s Mike Mayo: Citi My Top Pick of Big Banks
top_signals.summary: Investors are preparing for a significant earnings week as five of the six largest US banks, including JPMorgan, Citi, Wells Fargo, Bank of America, and Goldman Sachs, are scheduled to report their quarterly results within a few hours of each other. Mike Mayo, Head of US Large-Cap Bank Research at Wells Fargo, joined the program to share his outlook. He expects overall bank earnings to increase by 15 to 20 percent year-over-year, driven by a combination of strong Wall Street trading revenues and robust commercial loan growth on Main Street. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.
